sourceText: He overcame these trials through bold reforms, creating a standing army and subduing the feudal lords. He resettled Kipchak warriors to bolster his forces, culminating in the 'miraculous victory' at the Battle of Didgori (1121) against overwhelming odds, which paved the way for Georgia's Golden Age.
Without the will to be independent, no external help can rebuild a collapsed house.
It signifies the courage of proudly declaring resistance and independently reforming the country even in the midst of an absolute crisis.
More important than the number of an army is the unwavering mind of soldiers armed with conviction and firmly united.
It shows the determination that led to a miraculous victory with desperate resolve and tactics despite an overwhelming numerical disadvantage.
A true winner does not stop at conquering territory but blooms the flowers of tolerance and culture on the ruined land.
It means the governance that achieved the golden age of the country by encouraging learning and promoting harmony even after defeating the enemy with force.
